name: state-commitment
title: L1 State Commitment Protocol: Per-Enterprise Root Chains with Integrated ZK Verification
hypothesis: A StateCommitment.sol contract that maintains per-enterprise state root chains with integrated ZK proof verification can process batch submissions at < 300K gas, detect gaps and reversals in the root chain, and maintain complete batch history with < 500 bytes of storage per batch.
null_hypothesis: The combined cost of storage operations and ZK proof verification exceeds 300K gas, or per-batch storage exceeds 500 bytes, making a single-contract state commitment infeasible on Subnet-EVM.
predictions: P1: Integrated verification (single contract) uses < 300K gas per submission
P2: Delegated verification (cross-contract call to ZKVerifier) exceeds 300K gas due to redundant storage
P3: Minimal storage layout (root-only + events) uses < 100 bytes per batch
P4: Rich storage layout (root + packed metadata) uses < 200 bytes per batch
P5: Root chain continuity check catches 100% of gap and reversal attempts
